---
title: 'Darrell''s Writing Plan for 2025'
date: '2025-01-20T04:44:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: false
---

I've written three versions of my first novel, and ran into the same issues in the next two I tried, all three projects landing in the archive. I can write a compelling moment, but not a scene. I started studying the great writing teachers (Randy Ingermanson, KM Wieland, Brandon Sanderson, Ted Dekker, Bill Myers, and now I will add Hilary Layne to that list).&nbsp;<br />
<br />Brandon gave me permission to use what works and ignore what doesn't, for me. Each of them gave me tools I love.<p class="MsoNormal">Using all the tools I've gathered (see my YouTube <a href="https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLSbGHx9UCQ8oMXx3aBAZlv-Xf8Xet4dTG&amp;si=tYWPFxXWoeDbzgEa" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Storytelling</a> Playlist and my&nbsp;<a href="https://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/writing-craft-database.html">Writing StoryCraft Database</a>&nbsp;and this new content creator I found&nbsp;<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8738WWNvOLs&amp;list=PLSbGHx9UCQ8oMXx3aBAZlv-Xf8Xet4dTG&amp;index=50&amp;t=565s">Subplots Can't Hurt You (Rules, Principles, and Structure) - YouTube</a>&nbsp;AND&nbsp;<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RmhAGZJOf_o&amp;list=PLSbGHx9UCQ8oMXx3aBAZlv-Xf8Xet4dTG&amp;index=51">Professor Trailer | Rice University Precollege Online Program</a>).&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal">Hilary Layne at&nbsp;&nbsp;<a href="https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCBmqEPwc_8yJB-nhLjm9krw/about">The Second Story - YouTube</a>&nbsp;provides a better way to conceive of progress and a better way to conceive of a "first draft" as more of a sketch than actually "writing" the prose and dialogue and stuff.&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEjf8IaxVfOkc0lK4KoyZxEh0IsTbyj4GLTDizS8gXxfY1itqgdQCxYG3GySsno9s4Y1HaENNqVXXwa_cdn2yuvGgsX5E0F8u9naRJH6epoBOeOgr3dRg3fi-QvPBDv2CXGOziyFiB_jhaVJ1l4RG48DNxbx6-dDE9mG0Myga4A6p_c0q8ODA_UJ41tczKg" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img alt="" data-original-height="499" data-original-width="1133" height="141" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEjf8IaxVfOkc0lK4KoyZxEh0IsTbyj4GLTDizS8gXxfY1itqgdQCxYG3GySsno9s4Y1HaENNqVXXwa_cdn2yuvGgsX5E0F8u9naRJH6epoBOeOgr3dRg3fi-QvPBDv2CXGOziyFiB_jhaVJ1l4RG48DNxbx6-dDE9mG0Myga4A6p_c0q8ODA_UJ41tczKg" width="320" /></a></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEi2kMOJZMrl2ILV-CXEnS8Po7nMadufVYe9yeAyzuVI88fGQYj2jZIMvRR3gDOaqUWD1EXvsAfGJc8cTvwhfRAWUBKhWrxFWetkX6x6CClbb5VydHGNOD99qmx8xKC_kuemIJPq15YK-5QOeqxUAVZvX5zbJBUCpTSyt3iHoIumwMy8yYqA3tvq34eWT0w" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img alt="" data-original-height="352" data-original-width="1124" height="100" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEi2kMOJZMrl2ILV-CXEnS8Po7nMadufVYe9yeAyzuVI88fGQYj2jZIMvRR3gDOaqUWD1EXvsAfGJc8cTvwhfRAWUBKhWrxFWetkX6x6CClbb5VydHGNOD99qmx8xKC_kuemIJPq15YK-5QOeqxUAVZvX5zbJBUCpTSyt3iHoIumwMy8yYqA3tvq34eWT0w" width="320" /></a></div><br /><br /></div>So here is my writing plan for this year (in between other projects and life requirements).&nbsp;<p></p><p class="MsoNormal"></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Write plot mini-arcs like Layne teaches, and do so in VERY rough draft none artistic description ways. This is like when a pencil artist begins with broad shapes that don't look like much to a non-artist but are actually the frame upon which the art will evolve.</li><li>Using KM Weiland, I will be placing these arcs inside Scene/Sequel forms, with the sequel completing each arc.&nbsp;</li><li>Using I'm not sure which, or maybe all of them,&nbsp;</li><ul><li>Each scene must have a goal, even if that isn't the main story goal, but a smaller goal (Get coffee, sit in that chair, don't get involved in a discussion, and don't look crazy). This goal will be the primary goal throughout this mini-arc.</li><li>I will use the <b>Yes/But</b> or <b>No/And</b>&nbsp;to set beats in each scene for the goal, and ultimately end the Scene portion with a final&nbsp;<b>Yes/But</b>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<b>No/And</b>&nbsp;which will set up the Sequel, which will end the mini-arc.&nbsp;</li><li>The following arc must have a THEREFORE attached, but I may discover I actually needed more arcs between and create new chain links.</li><li></li></ul><li>Each session, I will review the previous sessions' work, edit whatever I need while also priming my brain back into that story world, and then I will set out to complete one more mini-arc. More if there is time and capacity, but one is the goal.</li><li>I will have things like the W story structure and the plot points from KM Weiland in the back of my mind, allowing for a few Yes/AND or No/But resolves at the appropriate points of the story, but most will follow the increasing tension pathway. Ultimately, these will probably be inserted after this first draft is done.&nbsp;</li><li>After a full first draft is done, including minor edits and plot reviews for adding missing mini-arcs, then I will go back and review the plot points, foreshadowing, and other items from story telling that flush out the story elements.&nbsp;</li><li>Only when this framework version is in a state that feels solid and complete, will I then go back to actually "Write" the scenes in proper prose, dialogue, and scene descriptions.&nbsp;</li></ul><div><br /></div><h4 style="text-align: left;">This is my template:&nbsp;</h4><p></p>

<h3 style="text-align: left;">Scene (Goal Conflict Disaster)</h3>

<h3 style="text-align: left;">Goal: He/She wantsâ€¦<br /> Beat 1 - Yes/But or No/Andâ€¦<br /> Beat 2 - Yes/But or No/Andâ€¦<br /> Beat 3 - Yes/But or No/Andâ€¦<br /> Sequel (Reaction Dilemma Decision)</h3>









<p class="MsoNormal">What is the dilemma and how does it force a decision?</p><b>
Examples here:&nbsp;<a href="https://www.darrellwolfe.com/2025/01/yesand-yesbut-noand-and-nobut-narrative.html">Yes/And, Yes/But, No/And, and No/But: Narrative Tools for Dynamic Storytelling ~ Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller</a><br /></b>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<div id="mc_embed_signup">
<form action="https://darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank">
<div id="mc_embed_signup_scroll">
<label>Subscribe Now - Pursue a No Hiding Lifestyle</label>&nbsp;<input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" required="" type="email" value="" /><br />
<div aria-hidden="true" style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;">
<input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" type="text" value="" /></div>
<div class="clear">
<input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div>
</div>
</form>
</div>
<br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom: Live Long and Prosper!</span><br />
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe (DG Wolfe)</span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant @&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/start-here-about_12.html" target="_blank">DarrellWolfe.com</a></span><br />
<br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/" target="_blank">Clifton StrengthsFinder</a>: Intellection, Learner,&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Ideation,</span><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">&nbsp;Achiever, Input</span><br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><a href="https://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" target="_blank">16Personalities</a> (Myers-Briggs Type): INFJ</span><br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><br /></span>
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><br /></span></div>
